description_long: This is a large, traditional wooden barn with a steeply pitched, shingled roof and a tall, narrow spire rising from the center, reminiscent of Eastern European rural architecture. The roof extends far beyond the walls, creating a wide overhang that shelters a wraparound porch supported by wooden posts. The exterior siding consists of closely set horizontal wooden planks, giving the structure a rustic and weathered appearance. There are no visible large barn doors or windows, suggesting the building may be used for storage or as a multi-purpose agricultural structure. The base of the barn is slightly elevated above the ground, with a wooden fence enclosing the porch area. The overall form and detailing evoke the style of historic wooden churches or barns found in regions such as Romania or Ukraine.
description: A large, steep-roofed wooden barn with a tall spire, wraparound porch, and horizontal plank siding, evoking traditional Eastern European rural architecture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long, steeply pitched roof, tall spire, and extensive wraparound porch with supporting posts.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the narrow width, the full height of the spire, and the symmetrical roof overhang.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the horizontal plank siding, porch, and the barn's elongated form.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back porch area, roof overhang, and the barn's overall symmetry.
attribute_roof_type: steeply pitched gable roof with a central spire
attribute_door_style: single hinged door, modestly sized, located on the porch side
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
